data.content_ref: inline:{"claim": "Harness strictly beats self-consistency iff e in (1/(k+1), k/(k+2)) AND p > p_min(e,r,k) := e*(k-1)/(k+1) / [e*(k-1)/(k+1) + (1-e)*r*(k+1)/(k-1)], with critic recall r in (0,1); the loss regime is p < p_min where false flags on correct answers exceed saved votes.", "mechanism": "Binomial majority-vote model: SC correctness is P[Bin(k,1-e)>k/2]; harness adds a filtering layer that removes TPs (bad candidates, prob r*e) and FPs (good candidates, prob (1-p)(1-e)). Net benefit requires FP removal rate < TP removal rate scaled by majority-margin. Differentiating shows the break-even precision p_min collapses to 0 as e approaches 1/(k+1) or k/(k+2), is maximized at the midpoint, and the window is nonempty for odd k>=5. Fixed-precision critics operating outside this window yield zero net benefit, explaining the repository's null results (e=0.033, 0.01 both far below the k=5 window of (0.167, 0.714)).", "scope": {"covers": ["odd k>=5", "base error 1/(k+1) < e < k/(k+2)", "critic precision p > p_min(e,r,k) for given recall r", "independent critic whose FP and TP rates are not question-adaptive"], "excludes": ["even k (tie-breaking complications)", "critic that adapts confidence per-question", "k<5 (majority too thin for robust filtering signal)", "e outside the fragility window"]}, "forbidden": [{"case": "Experiment produces e_hat within (0.17, 0.71) with p_hat > p_min(r_hat) but harness accuracy <= SC accuracy", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "Experiment produces p_hat < p_min(r_hat) yet harness accuracy strictly exceeds SC accuracy", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}, {"case": "Observed FP-removal rate on correct-majority questions exceeds 50% of observed TP-removal rate while harness still beats SC", "eval": "rubric:std-crit-design"}], "prose_notes": "Unifies 7811dbda and e977c688 by deriving the full 3D boundary surface p_min(e,r,k). The decisive experiment must scan both e and critic-threshold to hit the interior of the fragility window. Predicted p_min is lowest near window edges (easy to beat) and highest at the center (hardest to beat), so a threshold sweep is essential to find regions where available critic p exceeds p_min."}
